note: case_notes_structured embeds the per-sentence ground-truth labels used by the highlight-relevant and sequence-events formats. The identify-letter-type format embeds the correct answer in expected_answer_label.

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: suspected sleep apnoea referral
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a sleep clinic referral letter.

1. Mr Thomas Holm, aged 55, is a sales manager.
2. He has a six-month history of excessive daytime sleepiness.
3. His wife reports witnessed apnoeic episodes and habitual loud snoring.
4. He had two near-miss incidents while driving in the past 8 weeks.
5. Morning headaches present 3-4 mornings per week.
6. He is married with two adult children.
7. Body mass index 32, neck circumference 44 cm, Mallampati class III.
8. Epworth Sleepiness Scale 18; STOP-BANG 6/8.
9. He enjoys fishing at the weekends.
10. He has hypertension on amlodipine 10 mg daily.
11. He is on metformin 500 mg twice daily for type 2 diabetes.
12. He is an ex-smoker since 2019.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mr Thomas Holm, aged 55, is a sales manager.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Identification, age and occupation needed for routine referral opener (SC-001).

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He has a six-month history of excessive daytime sleepiness.
His wife reports witnessed apnoeic episodes and habitual loud snoring.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Witnessed feature; sleep clinic acts on this.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He had two near-miss incidents while driving in the past 8 weeks.
Morning headaches present 3-4 mornings per week.
He is married with two adult children.
Body mass index 32, neck circumference 44 cm, Mallampati class III.
Epworth Sleepiness Scale 18; STOP-BANG 6/8.
He enjoys fishing at the weekends.
He has hypertension on amlodipine 10 mg daily.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Frame with 'as you are aware'; sleep clinic may consider HTN-OSA link.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He is on metformin 500 mg twice daily for type 2 diabetes.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Comorbidity; brief mention via 'as you are aware'.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He is an ex-smoker since 2019.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Borderline; one short clause if word count allows.

drills.title: Identify letter type: ST elevation chest pain
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Mr Patel, aged 47, presented to walk-in surgery 90 minutes ago with crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw. Sweating, nausea. ECG: 1 mm ST elevation in II, III and aVF with reciprocal depression. Aspirin 300 mg given. 999 ambulance called.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to specialist unit
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Acute presentation, dangerous ECG findings, ambulance already called = urgent referral (LT-UR). The opening must include an explicit urgency marker per SC-003.

drills.title: Sequence events: pneumonia discharge journey
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Mrs Carter's admission, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of the event letters (e.g., C,B,A,D,E).

A. Apyrexial from day 3; oxygen weaned by day 4.
B. WCC 8.2, CRP 22 — repeat bloods on day 7.
C. Admission on 19 May 2026 with productive cough and pleuritic chest pain.
D. IV co-amoxiclav 1.2 g three times daily for 48 hours.
E. Discharged today on oral amoxicillin to complete 7-day course.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Chronological order: admission (C) → initial IV antibiotics (D) → apyrexial day 3, oxygen weaned day 4 (A) → repeat bloods day 7 (B) → discharge today (E).

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: post-pneumonia GP discharge
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a GP discharge letter after pneumonia. (Remember SC-020: no social history unless directly relevant to ongoing management.)

1. Mrs Carter was admitted on 19 May 2026 with community-acquired pneumonia.
2. She presented with a four-day history of productive cough.
3. Chest X-ray showed right lower zone consolidation.
4. Blood cultures grew Streptococcus pneumoniae sensitive to amoxicillin.
5. She received IV co-amoxiclav for 48 hours then oral amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily.
6. She works as a teacher and lives with her husband.
7. Her hobbies include gardening and book club.
8. She became apyrexial from day 3 and was weaned off oxygen by day 4.
9. She has been a non-smoker all her life.
10. She is being discharged on oral amoxicillin to complete a 7-day course.
11. A repeat chest X-ray is requested in 6 weeks to confirm resolution.
12. Renal function should be monitored in 2 weeks following IV antibiotics.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Carter was admitted on 19 May 2026 with community-acquired pneumonia.
She presented with a four-day history of productive cough.
Chest X-ray showed right lower zone consolidation.
Blood cultures grew Streptococcus pneumoniae sensitive to amoxicillin.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Justifies antibiotic choice for GP continuation.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She received IV co-amoxiclav for 48 hours then oral amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily.
She works as a teacher and lives with her husband.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Social history not relevant to ongoing pneumonia care (SC-020).

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Her hobbies include gardening and book club.
She became apyrexial from day 3 and was weaned off oxygen by day 4.
She has been a non-smoker all her life.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Social/lifestyle data, not relevant to ongoing pneumonia care.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She is being discharged on oral amoxicillin to complete a 7-day course.
A repeat chest X-ray is requested in 6 weeks to confirm resolution.
Renal function should be monitored in 2 weeks following IV antibiotics.

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: warfarin counselling letter
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a pharmacy-to-GP letter about Mrs Ahmed's warfarin counselling.

1. Mrs Bernadette Ahmed, aged 70, collected her first warfarin prescription today.
2. Warfarin was initiated following recent diagnosis of atrial fibrillation on 18 May 2026.
3. She has three grandchildren and visits them weekly.
4. She received 30 minutes of structured pharmacy counselling.
5. A yellow oral anticoagulant card and warfarin information booklet were issued.
6. She purchases ibuprofen 200 mg over the counter for chronic knee pain (3-4 tablets daily).
7. She enjoys baking and is a long-standing member of the local WI.
8. She has mild visual impairment and uses a magnifier to read her dosing book.
9. Carer assistance with monitoring entries was discussed.
10. She wears prescription reading glasses.
11. Action requested: review of her analgesia plan for a warfarin-compatible alternative.
12. Action requested: confirmation of INR monitoring arrangements (clinic vs practice nurse).

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Bernadette Ahmed, aged 70, collected her first warfarin prescription today.
Warfarin was initiated following recent diagnosis of atrial fibrillation on 18 May 2026.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Clinical context for the new prescription.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has three grandchildren and visits them weekly.
She received 30 minutes of structured pharmacy counselling.
A yellow oral anticoagulant card and warfarin information booklet were issued.
She purchases ibuprofen 200 mg over the counter for chronic knee pain (3-4 tablets daily).
case_notes_structured.rationale: High-risk interaction; drives the action request.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She enjoys baking and is a long-standing member of the local WI.
She has mild visual impairment and uses a magnifier to read her dosing book.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Affects safe medication management; drives carer-support request.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Carer assistance with monitoring entries was discussed.
She wears prescription reading glasses.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Detail captured already by 'mild visual impairment'.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Action requested: review of her analgesia plan for a warfarin-compatible alternative.
Action requested: confirmation of INR monitoring arrangements (clinic vs practice nurse).

drills.title: Identify letter type: insulin titration response
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** GP wrote on 19 May 2026 asking the pharmacist-led diabetes clinic to commence basal insulin titration for Mr Okonkwo (HbA1c 78 despite metformin and dapagliflozin). Today, 26 May 2026, Mr Okonkwo attended the clinic. Insulin glargine 12 units initiated; titration plan agreed; weekly telephone reviews booked. Writing to inform Dr Ahmed of action taken.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to specialist unit
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: The GP wrote with a specific question; you are replying with the action taken. That is a response letter (LT-RP). Open with 'Thank you for your letter of 19 May 2026 regarding Mr Okonkwo...'

drills.title: Sequence events: warfarin-naproxen interaction timeline
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Mr Williams' case, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of event letters.

A. Pharmacy clinical-check identified the warfarin-naproxen interaction; naproxen withheld.
B. Mr Williams received his most recent INR result of 2.6 on 18 May 2026.
C. Naproxen 500 mg twice daily was newly prescribed and dispensing began 5 days ago.
D. Mr Williams has noticed two episodes of gum bleeding while brushing teeth in the past 48 hours.
E. Priority INR recheck is booked for tomorrow.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Order: INR 2.6 on 18 May (B) → naproxen started 21 May (C) → gum bleeding 24-25 May (D) → today's pharmacy intervention (A) → INR booked tomorrow (E).

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: MDS adherence concern
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a pharmacy-to-GP letter about Mrs Lyle's adherence concerns.

1. Mrs Doris Lyle, aged 86, lives alone in sheltered housing.
2. She has used a weekly monitored dosage system since 2023.
3. Her favourite hobby is knitting.
4. The family raised concerns about her medication adherence.
5. She reports confusion about whether morning or evening doses have been taken.
6. Returned blister packs over the past 4 weeks have contained 3-6 unused doses per week.
7. She drinks two cups of tea every morning.
8. She has telephoned the pharmacy uncertain whether she has taken her morning dose.
9. She has had two falls in the past 2 months with one A&E attendance and no fracture.
10. She prefers brand-name biscuits to supermarket own brand.
11. Medicines in the dosage system include donepezil, apixaban, bisoprolol, lansoprazole and senna.
12. Action requested: formal cognitive assessment to inform safe medicines management.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Doris Lyle, aged 86, lives alone in sheltered housing.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Risk context relevant to medication management.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has used a weekly monitored dosage system since 2023.
Her favourite hobby is knitting.
The family raised concerns about her medication adherence.
She reports confusion about whether morning or evening doses have been taken.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Symptom of poor adherence and cognitive concern.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Returned blister packs over the past 4 weeks have contained 3-6 unused doses per week.
She drinks two cups of tea every morning.
She has telephoned the pharmacy uncertain whether she has taken her morning dose.
She has had two falls in the past 2 months with one A&E attendance and no fracture.
She prefers brand-name biscuits to supermarket own brand.
Medicines in the dosage system include donepezil, apixaban, bisoprolol, lansoprazole and senna.
Action requested: formal cognitive assessment to inform safe medicines management.

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: post-op knee discharge
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a nursing discharge letter to the community team.

1. Mrs Bell underwent elective right total knee replacement on 23 May 2026.
2. She tolerated spinal anaesthesia well with no intra-operative complications.
3. She enjoys watching Strictly Come Dancing.
4. By day 3 she had achieved independent transfers and passed stairs assessment.
5. Wound is clean and dry; dressing intact and to remain for 10 days from surgery.
6. She is discharged on tinzaparin 4500 units subcutaneously daily for 14 days.
7. Her husband is unable to administer injections due to needle phobia.
8. She has a documented allergy to penicillin which causes a rash.
9. She lives with her husband in a bungalow with ground-floor toilet.
10. She prefers Earl Grey tea to English Breakfast.
11. District nurse is requested to administer the daily tinzaparin for the remaining 10 days.
12. District nurse is requested to check the wound on day 7 and remove the dressing on day 10.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Bell underwent elective right total knee replacement on 23 May 2026.
She tolerated spinal anaesthesia well with no intra-operative complications.
She enjoys watching Strictly Come Dancing.
By day 3 she had achieved independent transfers and passed stairs assessment.
Wound is clean and dry; dressing intact and to remain for 10 days from surgery.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Wound status drives community wound care.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She is discharged on tinzaparin 4500 units subcutaneously daily for 14 days.
Her husband is unable to administer injections due to needle phobia.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Justifies why district nurse cover is needed.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has a documented allergy to penicillin which causes a rash.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Safety-critical for any future antibiotic prescribing.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She lives with her husband in a bungalow with ground-floor toilet.
She prefers Earl Grey tea to English Breakfast.
District nurse is requested to administer the daily tinzaparin for the remaining 10 days.
District nurse is requested to check the wound on day 7 and remove the dressing on day 10.

drills.title: Identify letter type: tissue viability handover
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Mr Adetola, aged 78, has a healing left venous leg ulcer. Hospital tissue viability service has stabilised the wound after a course of flucloxacillin for cellulitis. He is being handed over to the community nursing team for ongoing twice-weekly 4-layer compression dressings. He remains under wound-care management; this is not a discharge home with no follow-up.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to another team
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Patient remains under wound-care management; the acute tissue viability team is handing over to the community nursing team. That is a transfer (LT-TR), not a discharge.

drills.title: Sequence events: elderly falls admission
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Mrs Pearson's recent history, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of event letters.

A. Admission on 18 May 2026 following the third unwitnessed fall in 2 months, with fractured neck of left humerus.
B. Fall on 8 March 2026 — A&E attendance for 'long lie', no fracture.
C. Fall on 14 April 2026 — resulted in colles fracture treated in plaster.
D. Occupational therapy home visit on 24 May 2026 identified multiple environmental hazards.
E. Refused offer of overnight carer at previous discharge in April 2026.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Order: long-lie fall 8 March (B) → colles fracture fall 14 April (C) → refused overnight carer at the April discharge (E) → current admission 18 May (A) → OT home visit 24 May (D).

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: medication-overuse migraine referral
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a neurology referral letter for Mrs Wallis.

1. Mrs Catherine Wallis, aged 44, is a primary school teacher.
2. She has a three-year history of migraine that has escalated to 22-25 headache days per month over the past four months.
3. She enjoys yoga at the weekends.
4. She uses codeine-containing analgesia four to five days per week.
5. Her two children attend the same school where she teaches.
6. She uses sumatriptan PRN at four to six doses per month.
7. Fundoscopy revealed no papilloedema; cranial nerve examination was unremarkable.
8. Past medical history includes migraine with aura since 2018 and anxiety on sertraline 50 mg daily.
9. She prefers loose-leaf tea to teabags.
10. Triggers identified include poor sleep, missed meals and work stress.
11. Iron-deficiency anaemia in 2024 has resolved.
12. Gradual codeine reduction has been advised and a headache diary will be continued.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Catherine Wallis, aged 44, is a primary school teacher.
She has a three-year history of migraine that has escalated to 22-25 headache days per month over the past four months.
She enjoys yoga at the weekends.
She uses codeine-containing analgesia four to five days per week.
Her two children attend the same school where she teaches.
She uses sumatriptan PRN at four to six doses per month.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Acute therapy detail; informs preventative-therapy decision.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Fundoscopy revealed no papilloedema; cranial nerve examination was unremarkable.
Past medical history includes migraine with aura since 2018 and anxiety on sertraline 50 mg daily.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Anxiety is comorbid driver; sertraline relevant for prescribing decisions.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She prefers loose-leaf tea to teabags.
Triggers identified include poor sleep, missed meals and work stress.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Useful context; can be brief mention.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Iron-deficiency anaemia in 2024 has resolved.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Borderline relevance; can omit if word count tight.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Gradual codeine reduction has been advised and a headache diary will be continued.

drills.title: Identify letter type: uveitis with suspected ankylosing spondylitis
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Mr Quinn, aged 36, walk-in surgery, 48-hour painful red right eye with cells in anterior chamber and small hypopyon. Marked photophobia, visual acuity reduced. Family history of HLA-B27 positive ankylosing spondylitis; Schober's test limited and bilateral sacroiliac tenderness today. Topical cyclopentolate applied; on-call ophthalmology registrar alerted; same-day appointment given.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to specialist unit
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Acute eye presentation with hypopyon, marked photophobia and reduced visual acuity, with on-call team already alerted and same-day appointment — urgent referral (LT-UR). Opening must include an urgency marker per SC-003.

drills.title: Sequence events: pulmonary embolus admission timeline
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Mrs Rashid's admission, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of event letters.

A. CT pulmonary angiogram confirmed segmental pulmonary embolus in the right lower lobe.
B. Apixaban 10 mg twice daily commenced on day 1.
C. Long-haul flight five days before admission.
D. Oxygen weaned by day 3; saturations 97 percent on room air.
E. Discharged today on apixaban with step-down dose change on day 7 of treatment.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Order: long-haul flight 18 May (C) → CTPA confirms PE on admission 23 May (A) → apixaban commenced day 1 (B) → oxygen weaned by day 3 (D) → discharge today 26 May with step-down on day 7 (E).

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: paediatric eczema referral
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a paediatric dermatology referral for Master Hughes.

1. Master Oliver Hughes, aged seven, has atopic eczema poorly controlled by primary-care treatment.
2. He has had three significant flares in the past six months, each requiring oral prednisolone.
3. He likes playing football at school.
4. He has had four school absences in the past month attributable to eczema flares.
5. His birthday is in July.
6. Examination revealed extensive flexural lichenification, dry skin and two areas of superficial impetiginisation on the right forearm.
7. He has mild intermittent asthma on as-required salbutamol and seasonal allergic rhinitis.
8. Family history is positive for atopic eczema in his mother and maternal grandmother.
9. His older sister has braces on her teeth.
10. Current treatment includes emollient twice daily with limited adherence, topical hydrocortisone for mild flares and topical betamethasone valerate for severe flares.
11. He attends Holly Lane Primary School.
12. Topical fusidic acid has been started for the impetiginised areas for five days.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Master Oliver Hughes, aged seven, has atopic eczema poorly controlled by primary-care treatment.
He has had three significant flares in the past six months, each requiring oral prednisolone.
He likes playing football at school.
He has had four school absences in the past month attributable to eczema flares.
His birthday is in July.
Examination revealed extensive flexural lichenification, dry skin and two areas of superficial impetiginisation on the right forearm.
He has mild intermittent asthma on as-required salbutamol and seasonal allergic rhinitis.
Family history is positive for atopic eczema in his mother and maternal grandmother.
His older sister has braces on her teeth.
Current treatment includes emollient twice daily with limited adherence, topical hydrocortisone for mild flares and topical betamethasone valerate for severe flares.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Treatment ladder context; informs specialist plan.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He attends Holly Lane Primary School.
case_notes_structured.rationale: School name not relevant unless safeguarding involved.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Topical fusidic acid has been started for the impetiginised areas for five days.

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: salbutamol overuse pharmacy letter
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a pharmacy letter to the GP about Miss Reilly's salbutamol overuse.

1. Miss Hannah Reilly, aged 24, presented at the pharmacy today requesting two simultaneous salbutamol inhalers.
2. She has collected twelve salbutamol inhalers in the past twelve months.
3. Her favourite TV show is on Netflix.
4. Her beclometasone preventer inhaler has been markedly under-collected (one inhaler in twelve months).
5. She reports using salbutamol several times daily, with up to ten puffs in an hour during attacks.
6. She has had two A&E attendances in the past six months for acute asthma exacerbations.
7. She lives in a flat with two cats; she cycles to work three days per week.
8. She has stopped using her beclometasone inhaler because she perceives it does not help quickly.
9. She has nocturnal cough and breathlessness two to three nights per week.
10. She smokes ten cigarettes per day and vapes additionally.
11. She often shops at the local supermarket on Saturday mornings.
12. Two simultaneous salbutamol inhalers were refused today; one inhaler was supplied with verbal advice and inhaler technique was checked.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Miss Hannah Reilly, aged 24, presented at the pharmacy today requesting two simultaneous salbutamol inhalers.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Drives the letter; identifies patient and event.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has collected twelve salbutamol inhalers in the past twelve months.
Her favourite TV show is on Netflix.
Her beclometasone preventer inhaler has been markedly under-collected (one inhaler in twelve months).
She reports using salbutamol several times daily, with up to ten puffs in an hour during attacks.
She has had two A&E attendances in the past six months for acute asthma exacerbations.
She lives in a flat with two cats; she cycles to work three days per week.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Cat dander as trigger is relevant; cycling not.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has stopped using her beclometasone inhaler because she perceives it does not help quickly.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Explains preventer under-use; informs counselling approach.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has nocturnal cough and breathlessness two to three nights per week.
She smokes ten cigarettes per day and vapes additionally.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Modifiable risk factor; drives cessation referral.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She often shops at the local supermarket on Saturday mornings.
Two simultaneous salbutamol inhalers were refused today; one inhaler was supplied with verbal advice and inhaler technique was checked.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Action taken at pharmacy; SC-018 requires explicit request from pharmacist.

drills.title: Identify letter type: methotrexate dosing query
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Mr Nowak, aged 55, is on cycle 4 of high-dose methotrexate for CNS prophylaxis of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma. Cycle 5 scheduled in two days. Pre-cycle clinical-check today identified deteriorating renal function (eGFR fallen from 78 to 49 over three cycles). Cycle 4 methotrexate plasma level was above target; folinic acid rescue extended. Writing to oncology pharmacist to request review of cycle 5 dosing and rescue plan.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to specialist unit
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Hospital pharmacy alerting oncology pharmacy to a clinical-check concern — non-medical / communication letter (LT-NM). The request must be stated explicitly in the opening per SC-018.

drills.title: Sequence events: SSRI hyponatraemia timeline
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Mrs Murphy's case, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of event letters.

A. Citalopram dose increased from 20 mg to 40 mg daily.
B. Sodium 138 mmol/L on routine review.
C. Two unwitnessed falls within the past 10 days.
D. Sodium dropped to 128 mmol/L on community phlebotomy check.
E. Pharmacy withheld dispensing of citalopram 40 mg pending GP review.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Order: sodium 138 on 28 April (B) → citalopram uptitrated 12 May (A) → sodium 128 on 22 May (D) → falls 16-26 May (C) → pharmacy intervention today 26 May (E).

drills.title: Identify letter type: post-MI medication optimisation reply
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Dr Khan (GP) wrote on 19 May asking cardiology pharmacy clinic to review Mr McKenzie's post-MI medications, especially statin intolerance. Mr McKenzie attended clinic today; atorvastatin reduced from 80 mg to 40 mg; ezetimibe added; 6-week follow-up arranged. Writing to inform Dr Khan of plan agreed.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to specialist unit
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: GP asked a specific question; reply summarising the plan agreed = response letter (LT-RP). Open with 'Thank you for your letter of 19 May 2026 regarding Mr McKenzie...'

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: simple UTI discharge to GP
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a nursing discharge letter to the GP for Mrs Olson.

1. Mrs Beatrice Olson was admitted on 24 May 2026 with acute confusion in the context of a urinary tract infection.
2. Mid-stream urine grew Escherichia coli sensitive to nitrofurantoin.
3. She has a Persian cat called Misty.
4. She received intravenous ceftriaxone for 24 hours, followed by oral nitrofurantoin 100 mg twice daily.
5. Her cognitive function returned to baseline by day 2.
6. She enjoys watching daytime television.
7. She is discharged on oral nitrofurantoin to complete a 5-day course with three days remaining.
8. Past medical history includes mild cognitive impairment on donepezil, atrial fibrillation on apixaban and hypertension on amlodipine.
9. She has a documented penicillin allergy, which caused a rash at age 18.
10. Her favourite biscuits are custard creams.
11. Her daughter will stay with her for the next five days to provide supervision.
12. GP review at two weeks is requested to assess full cognitive recovery.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mrs Beatrice Olson was admitted on 24 May 2026 with acute confusion in the context of a urinary tract infection.
Mid-stream urine grew Escherichia coli sensitive to nitrofurantoin.
She has a Persian cat called Misty.
She received intravenous ceftriaxone for 24 hours, followed by oral nitrofurantoin 100 mg twice daily.
Her cognitive function returned to baseline by day 2.
She enjoys watching daytime television.
She is discharged on oral nitrofurantoin to complete a 5-day course with three days remaining.
Past medical history includes mild cognitive impairment on donepezil, atrial fibrillation on apixaban and hypertension on amlodipine.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Frame via 'as you are aware' (SC-023); GP knows already.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: She has a documented penicillin allergy, which caused a rash at age 18.
Her favourite biscuits are custard creams.
Her daughter will stay with her for the next five days to provide supervision.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Discharge safety plan relevant to GP.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: GP review at two weeks is requested to assess full cognitive recovery.

drills.title: Identify letter type: voluntary mental health admission
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Read the case notes. From the options below, choose the correct letter type.

**Case notes summary:** Mr Hartley, aged 29, with first-episode psychosis since 2024 currently on aripiprazole. Crisis team intensive support for past 14 days; mental state has deteriorated despite dose increase and twice-daily visits. Today: voluntary admission to acute inpatient unit agreed with patient and mother. Patient remains under specialist mental health care; care is being handed across to inpatient team.

**Options:**
A. LT-RR — Routine referral
B. LT-UR — Urgent referral
C. LT-DG — Discharge to GP
D. LT-TR — Transfer to another team
E. LT-RP — Response
F. LT-NM — Non-medical communication
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Patient remains under specialist mental health care; crisis team is handing across to inpatient team — transfer letter (LT-TR). Care is not being returned to primary care (which would be discharge).

drills.title: Sequence events: paediatric anaphylaxis history
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Below are five events from Ethan Bryant's allergy history, in random order. Put them in chronological order from earliest to latest. Reply as a comma-separated list of event letters.

A. Skin-prick testing and specific IgE confirmed peanut allergy with high risk for systemic reaction.
B. Anaphylactic episode at a friend's birthday party with trace peanut exposure, treated with adrenaline auto-injector.
C. Updated allergy action plan for school setting issued today.
D. A&E observation following adrenaline auto-injector use; no intensive care required.
E. Family discussion in clinic today reinforced two adrenaline auto-injectors at all times and updated school plan.
drills.expected_answer_explanation: Order: diagnostic testing first (A) → anaphylactic episode November 2025 (B) → A&E observation same admission (D) → updated action plan today (C) → family discussion in clinic today (E).

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: post-stroke continence referral
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a continence service referral for Mr Wilson.

1. Mr Henry Wilson, aged 77, has had urinary incontinence persisting eight weeks following a right MCA ischaemic stroke.
2. Residual deficits include left arm weakness and mild cognitive slowing.
3. He likes to garden in his front yard.
4. He has three to four daytime and one to two nocturnal incontinence episodes daily.
5. He used to be a postman before retirement.
6. The pattern is mixed urge and functional incontinence.
7. Urinalysis today was negative and post-void residual scan was 60 mL.
8. He has had two episodes of sacral pressure-area redness in the past month related to incontinence.
9. His wife is the primary carer and reports increasing fatigue from frequent toileting assistance.
10. He prefers black coffee in the morning.
11. He had initially declined continence pads but accepted them today after discussion.
12. Antimuscarinic medications carry cognitive risk in post-stroke patients and require specialist consideration.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mr Henry Wilson, aged 77, has had urinary incontinence persisting eight weeks following a right MCA ischaemic stroke.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Referral opener anchor (SC-001) and core clinical context.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Residual deficits include left arm weakness and mild cognitive slowing.
He likes to garden in his front yard.
He has three to four daytime and one to two nocturnal incontinence episodes daily.
He used to be a postman before retirement.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Past occupation; not relevant to continence referral.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: The pattern is mixed urge and functional incontinence.
Urinalysis today was negative and post-void residual scan was 60 mL.
He has had two episodes of sacral pressure-area redness in the past month related to incontinence.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Skin integrity at risk; urgent for specialist input.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: His wife is the primary carer and reports increasing fatigue from frequent toileting assistance.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Carer burden; relevant to service planning.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He prefers black coffee in the morning.
He had initially declined continence pads but accepted them today after discussion.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Recent action; informs specialist's starting point.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Antimuscarinic medications carry cognitive risk in post-stroke patients and require specialist consideration.

drills.title: Highlight relevant info: palliative care transfer
drills.case_notes_markdown:
Click each sentence that you would INCLUDE in a nursing transfer letter to the hospice.

1. Mr Janssen has end-stage metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma.
2. He was diagnosed in November 2025.
3. He completed 4 cycles of FOLFIRINOX before discontinuation due to poor tolerance.
4. He used to enjoy cycling and was a keen amateur cyclist in his 40s.
5. He was admitted on 21 May 2026 with severe abdominal pain and nausea.
6. His performance status has declined from ECOG 2 to ECOG 3 during this admission.
7. His wife is his primary carer; family aware of prognosis and supportive of hospice transfer.
8. A DNACPR form was completed on 23 May 2026.
9. His pain is rated 7 out of 10 at rest, 9 out of 10 on movement.
10. His son recently moved house.
11. Current medications include morphine sulphate modified release 60 mg twice daily and PRN immediate release.
12. Anti-emetic review, bowel regimen review and anxiety management are requested.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Mr Janssen has end-stage metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma.
He was diagnosed in November 2025.
He completed 4 cycles of FOLFIRINOX before discontinuation due to poor tolerance.
He used to enjoy cycling and was a keen amateur cyclist in his 40s.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Hobby trivia, not relevant to palliative care plan.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: He was admitted on 21 May 2026 with severe abdominal pain and nausea.
His performance status has declined from ECOG 2 to ECOG 3 during this admission.
His wife is his primary carer; family aware of prognosis and supportive of hospice transfer.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Family situation directly relevant for hospice support.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: A DNACPR form was completed on 23 May 2026.
His pain is rated 7 out of 10 at rest, 9 out of 10 on movement.
His son recently moved house.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Family trivia not relevant to care.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Current medications include morphine sulphate modified release 60 mg twice daily and PRN immediate release.
case_notes_structured.rationale: Current pain regimen — basis for hospice optimisation.

case_notes_structured.sentence_text: Anti-emetic review, bowel regimen review and anxiety management are requested.
